verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (BiP) is a canonical ER-luminal chaperone with KDEL retention, lacking a TM domain at baseline. However, it undergoes well-documented stress-induced and cancer-cell-induced externalization to the outer leaflet via non-classical secretion and ER-PM translocation. Ecto-HSPA5 is detected on tumor cells and during cell death (apoptosis, ICD), where it serves as a DAMP and immunogenic target. This stress / cancer-state-dependent surface pool qualifies as contextual surface accessibility.
